title: Categorize assets
user_story: As a Company Admin, I want to organize every asset into a defined category, so that inventory is structured, searchable, and reportable by type.
acceptance_criteria: Given I am registering or editing an asset, when I set its category, then I must choose exactly one of IT Equipment, Mobile Devices, Network Equipment, Peripherals, Furniture, Security & Access, Software Licenses, or Other Equipment; Given category is a required attribute, when I try to save an asset without a category, then the system blocks the save and prompts me to select one; Given an asset is saved, when I view the inventory list or asset detail, then each asset displays its assigned category; Given I filter or search the inventory, when I select one or more categories, then only assets in the chosen categories are shown; Given I run inventory reports, when I group by category, then asset counts and states roll up per category.

title: Track asset lifecycle states
user_story: As a Company Admin, I want each asset to move through the defined lifecycle states, so that I always know the current status of every asset and only valid transitions occur.
acceptance_criteria: Given an asset exists, when I view it, then its state is exactly one of Available, Allocated, Issued, In Repair, Returned, Retired, or Disposed; Given an asset transaction or status change occurs, when the change is recorded, then the asset's lifecycle state is updated automatically and consistently with the transaction (e.g. Issue -> Issued, Return -> Returned); Given a newly registered asset, when no transaction has occurred, then its default state is Available; Given I review inventory, when I filter by one or more states, then only assets in those lifecycle states are listed; Given any state change, when it is applied, then the prior and new state, actor, and timestamp are retained in the asset history.

title: Issue an asset to an employee
user_story: As a Company Admin, I want to issue an available asset to a workforce member, so that the item is formally allocated and tracked to that person.
acceptance_criteria: Given an asset in an issuable state (Available or Allocated), when I record an Issue transaction to an employee, then the asset is linked to that employee and its state changes to Issued; Given I attempt to issue an asset that is already Issued, In Repair, Retired, or Disposed, when I submit, then the system prevents the issue and explains why; Given an issue transaction is completed, when I view the asset history, then the issue event is logged with the recipient employee, the acting admin, and the date; Given an asset is issued, when the recipient views their profile, then the issued asset appears in their allocated-assets list; Given an asset is issued, when acknowledgement is required, then a pending acknowledgement is raised for the recipient.

title: Return an asset
user_story: As a Company Admin, I want to record the return of an issued asset with a condition check, so that it can be reassessed and returned to inventory.
acceptance_criteria: Given an issued asset, when I record a Return transaction, then the asset is unlinked from the employee and its state is updated to Returned; Given a return is recorded, when I view the asset history, then the return event is logged with date, returning employee, and acting admin; Given the returned asset is reassessed as serviceable, when I complete the assessment, then it can transition back to Available for reissue; Given the returned asset requires repair, when I flag it, then it can transition to In Repair; Given a return is recorded, when a return condition assessment exists, then it is stored against the asset record.

title: Transfer an asset between employees
user_story: As a Company Admin, I want to transfer an asset from one employee to another, so that reassignment is tracked without a full return and reissue.
acceptance_criteria: Given an allocated or issued asset, when I record a Transfer transaction to a new employee, then the asset's ownership moves to the new employee while remaining in an issued/allocated state; Given a transfer is completed, when I view the asset history, then both the source and destination employees, the acting admin, and the transfer date are recorded; Given a transfer occurs, when it completes, then the source employee's allocated-assets list no longer shows the asset and the destination employee's list now shows it; Given acknowledgement is required, when the transfer completes, then a new pending acknowledgement is raised for the destination employee.

title: Recover an asset
user_story: As a Company Admin, I want to record recovery of an outstanding asset, so that unreturned items are reclaimed and accounted for.
acceptance_criteria: Given an asset held by an employee (typically during exit or when overdue), when I record a Recovery transaction, then the asset is marked as recovered, unlinked from the employee, and reflected in its lifecycle state; Given a recovery is recorded, when I view the asset history, then the recovery event is logged with date, employee, and acting admin; Given an asset is recovered, when the exit or overdue follow-up is reviewed, then it is no longer listed as outstanding for that employee; Given a recovered asset is assessed, when it is serviceable, then it can transition to Available.

title: Digitally acknowledge asset receipt
user_story: As an Employee (User), I want to digitally acknowledge receipt of an asset with a condition assessment, so that there is a clear record of what I received and its state at handover.
acceptance_criteria: Given an asset is issued or transferred to me, when I open the acknowledgement, then I can record a digital acknowledgement of receipt together with a condition assessment of the asset; Given I submit the acknowledgement, when it is saved, then it is timestamped and linked to me and the specific asset; Given I have acknowledged receipt, when the record is viewed, then the pending acknowledgement clears and the acknowledged condition is visible on the asset record; Given I have not yet acknowledged, when the pending-acknowledgements report is run, then the asset appears as awaiting my acknowledgement.

title: Digitally acknowledge asset return
user_story: As an Employee (User), I want to digitally acknowledge return of an asset with a condition assessment, so that the condition at return is documented and disputes are avoided.
acceptance_criteria: Given I am returning an asset issued to me, when I complete the return acknowledgement, then my digital acknowledgement and a condition assessment of the asset are captured; Given the return acknowledgement is saved, when the asset is reviewed, then the return condition and acknowledgement timestamp are available on the asset record and linked to me; Given a return acknowledgement is outstanding, when the pending-acknowledgements report is run, then the return appears as awaiting acknowledgement; Given the return condition differs from the receipt condition, when the record is reviewed, then both condition assessments are visible for comparison.

title: Integrate asset actions with onboarding and exit workflows
user_story: As a Company Admin, I want asset issuance and recovery to be integrated with onboarding and exit workflows, so that assets are handled automatically at the right point in an employee's lifecycle.
acceptance_criteria: Given an employee is onboarding, when the onboarding workflow runs, then asset issuance tasks are triggered as part of the workflow; Given an employee is exiting, when the exit workflow runs, then asset recovery tasks are triggered as part of the workflow; Given assets remain outstanding, when the exit workflow is reviewed or completed, then unrecovered assets are surfaced and block or flag exit clearance for follow-up; Given an asset issuance or recovery task completes, when the workflow is viewed, then the linked asset transaction and its status are reflected in the workflow step.

title: Report on asset assignment, inventory, and outstanding items
user_story: As a Company Admin, I want reports on asset assignment, inventory, pending acknowledgements, and overdue returns, so that I can monitor asset usage and follow up on exceptions.
acceptance_criteria: Given assets exist, when I run the asset assignment report, then I see which assets are assigned to which employees with issue dates; Given assets exist, when I run the inventory report, then I see all assets with their categories and lifecycle states; Given acknowledgements are pending, when I run the pending acknowledgements report, then I see all assets awaiting receipt or return acknowledgement with the responsible employee; Given returns are due, when I run the overdue returns report, then I see all assets whose expected return date has passed, with the holder and days overdue; Given any report is generated, when I apply filters (category, state, employee, date range), then results are scoped accordingly and can be exported.

title: Register and maintain assets
user_story: As a Company Admin, I want to register new assets and maintain their details, so that the inventory accurately reflects owned equipment available for allocation.
acceptance_criteria: Given I add a new asset, when I complete required attributes including category, then the asset is created with a default state of Available; Given an asset exists, when I edit its details, then the changes are saved and the edit is recorded in the asset history; Given an asset is registered, when I view inventory, then it becomes available for Issue transactions; Given a required attribute is missing, when I try to save, then the system blocks the save and identifies the missing fields.

title: Send assets for repair and back to service
user_story: As a Company Admin, I want to move an asset into and out of the In Repair state, so that unserviceable equipment is tracked and only serviceable assets are reissued.
acceptance_criteria: Given an issued or returned asset needs servicing, when I set it to In Repair, then its lifecycle state changes to In Repair and it is excluded from issuable inventory; Given an asset is In Repair, when repair completes and it is serviceable, then I can transition it back to Available; Given an asset is In Repair and irreparable, when I decide, then I can transition it to Retired; Given any repair transition, when it is applied, then the event, date, and acting admin are logged in the asset history.

title: Retire and dispose of assets
user_story: As a Company Admin, I want to retire and dispose of assets at end of life, so that inventory reflects only usable equipment and disposal is auditable.
acceptance_criteria: Given an asset is no longer in service (e.g. returned, recovered, or In Repair and irreparable), when I set it to Retired, then it is removed from issuable inventory and its state is Retired; Given a Retired asset, when it is physically disposed of, then I can transition it to Disposed; Given an asset is Disposed, when I attempt any allocation transaction, then the system prevents it; Given a retire or dispose action, when it is applied, then the event, reason, date, and acting admin are logged in the asset history and reflected in inventory reports.

title: Record acknowledgement on behalf of non-user employees
user_story: As a Company Admin, I want to capture asset receipt and return acknowledgements with condition assessments on behalf of Employee (Non-User) workforce members, so that assets issued to staff without system access are still fully documented.
acceptance_criteria: Given an asset is issued to an Employee (Non-User) who cannot log in, when I record the acknowledgement on their behalf, then the digital acknowledgement and condition assessment are captured and linked to that employee and asset; Given I acknowledge on behalf of an employee, when the record is saved, then it is timestamped and identifies me as the recording admin as well as the employee; Given the non-user employee returns an asset, when I record the return acknowledgement with condition assessment, then the return condition is stored on the asset record; Given acknowledgements are captured on their behalf, when the pending-acknowledgements report is run, then those assets do not appear as outstanding.

title: View my allocated assets
user_story: As an Employee (User), I want to view the assets currently allocated to me and their acknowledgement status, so that I know what I hold and what actions I still owe.
acceptance_criteria: Given assets are issued or transferred to me, when I open my asset list, then I see each asset with its category, issue date, and current lifecycle state; Given an acknowledgement is pending for me, when I view the asset, then it is flagged as awaiting receipt or return acknowledgement with an action to complete it; Given an asset return is overdue, when I view my list, then the overdue status is indicated; Given I have no allocated assets, when I open my asset list, then an empty state is shown.

title: Oversee asset inventory across companies in the group
user_story: As a Group Company Admin, I want consolidated visibility of asset inventory, assignments, and outstanding items across the companies in my group, so that I can monitor asset usage and exceptions group-wide.
acceptance_criteria: Given multiple companies exist in my group, when I run the inventory or assignment report, then I can view assets across all companies in my group with company as a filter/grouping dimension; Given outstanding items exist, when I run the pending acknowledgements or overdue returns reports, then I see exceptions aggregated across my group's companies; Given I have group-level scope, when I view any company's assets, then I see them read-consistently but operational transactions remain governed by each Company Admin's scope; Given I filter by a single company, when I apply it, then results are limited to that company.
